meaning: adj.未被通知的,未获情报,无生气的
examples: The public is generally uninformed about these diseases.
If good people do nothing there is a real possibility that an uninformed citizenry will extinguish the precious embers of medical progress.
"To be uninformed is to be slow," he said.
Since the NASD blocked me in court from publishing it, investors remain perilously uninformed.
Almost all of the recent sales have come from these relatively uninformed large shareholders.
